background: Say whether each of the boldface numbers is a parameter or a statistic.

task.query: Insured? A data set based on 168 hospital discharge summaries shows that 20% of patients were uninsured. (The review takes place in a large referral hospital.)

Percent African American. Data from a standard metropolitan area (SMA) indicate that 12% of the inhabitants are African American. A telephone survey that was based on a random-digit-dialing machine found that 8% of respondents were African American.
task.answer: The number 12% is a parameter. The number 8% is a statistic.

task.query: Cost of antihypertensive medication. Data from 10 online pharmacies reveal that the average cost of a 1-month supply of a particular medication is $31.20 with standard deviation $7.75. Data from 10 community pharmacies shows an average cost of $33.18 with standard deviation $7.88.
task.answer: All are statistics. (These statistics will be used to infer costs at online and community pharmacies, respectively.)
